title: Compare all the players who have played Druid across all available runs. Use only metrics from time logged on Druid. Compare only players who are members of FAFO guild. Prioritize Healing Per Second > Cleanses Per Second > Downed Healing Per Second > Outgoing CC > Outgoing Conditions. Player downs and deaths count as minimum negative. Minimum Sample of 2 runs per player. Create a table with weighted results

turns.userText: Compare all the players who have played Druid across all available runs. Use only metrics from time logged on Druid. Compare only players who are members of FAFO guild. Prioritize Healing Per Second > Cleanses Per Second > Downed Healing Per Second > Outgoing CC > Outgoing Conditions. Player downs and deaths count as minimum negative. Minimum Sample of 2 runs per player. Create a table with weighted results
turns.agentText:
I'll pull the runs list, guild membership, and full attendance data all at once to get started.

Good. Now I need the FAFO guild member roster, plus identify all Druid players. Let me pull guild members and also start pulling player stats for the key Druid players.

Now I know the FAFO Druid candidates. Let me pull all the needed performance sections simultaneously.

Good — player_stats is aggregate data. Now I need downed healing, CC out, and conditions out across all runs. Let me query the rollup and pull those sections simultaneously.

Now let me get aggregate downed healing across all runs from the summaries, and also confirm Druid-run-level professionBreakdown properly.

Good — Druid healing and cleanses confirmed per-run. The downed/CC/conditions fields aren't in summaries. Let me probe the actual summary player fields.

The fields I need: `classTimes` for Druid-specific time, plus `deaths` and `downs`. Let me aggregate those from Druid-profession rows.

Good — `classTimes` is an array. Now I can aggregate precisely.

Now I have exact Druid-only healing, cleanses, deaths, downs, and combat time per player. For DHPS, CC, and conditions I need one more Druid-era run. Let me pull the most useful Agg run.

I have exact Druid-run aggregate data for HPS, CPS, deaths, and downs. Now one more pull to capture downed healing for the historical-Druid players.

Now I have everything. Let me compile the full weighted table.

**Aeri.4529 leads the FAFO Druid pool with 83.5/90 — the highest raw HPS (2,111) in the group and the cleanest survivability record (3.0 deaths/run, 4.5 downs/run) across 6 Druid runs.**

---

### Methodology

All metrics are filtered to **runs where the player's logged profession was Druid** (exact profession from `classTimes` in run summaries), sourced from both Fafo-Agg and Fafo-June repos. Minimun 2 Druid runs required; non-FAFO members excluded.

**Weights (max 90 pts):** HPS 35 · CPS 25 · DH/s 20 · CC/run 10 · Deaths/run −5 max · Downs/run −5 max. All five metrics are min-max normalised within the FAFO Druid pool. DH/s and CC/run are from per-run section data (not in summaries); three players have no Druid run captured in the sampling windows — their DH/s and CC/run are imputed at the pool median (DH/s 173.4, CC 58.75). Conditions Out had <3 players with Druid-run data and is excluded from scoring. Deaths and downs penalties are "minimum negative" — capped at −5 pts each so they act as a tiebreaker rather than a dominant factor.

---

### Full Data + Weighted Scores

| Rank | Player | Druid Runs | Druid Combat | HPS | CPS | DH/s | CC/run | Deaths/run | Downs/run | **Score /90** |
|------|--------|:----------:|:------------:|----:|----:|-----:|-------:|:----------:|:---------:|:-------------:|
| 1 | **Aeri.4529** | 6 | 4.9h | **2,111** | 1.003 | **222.9** | 70* | **3.0** | **4.5** | **83.5** |
| 2 | **DraGonfly.9317** ⚑ | 11 | 9.2h | 2,094 | 1.058 | 173.4⚑ | 58.75⚑ | 4.1 | 5.7 | **71.9** |
| 3 | **Astronauta.1087** † | 2 | 1.7h | 2,059 | **1.142** | 165.0* | 64* | 6.0 | 9.0 | **68.5** |
| 4 | **Delightful Denali.1053** | 15 | 11.9h | 1,457 | 0.862 | 181.8* | **73*** | 4.7 | 7.2 | **51.0** |
| 5 | **Genki.3125** ⚑ | 7 | 4.8h | 1,112 | 0.997 | 173.4⚑ | 58.75⚑ | 4.9 | 7.7 | **43.1** |
| 6 | **Eliare.4069** | 25 | 19.6h | 1,228 | 0.680 | 190.9 | 53.5 | 6.2 | **10.0** | **30.0** |
| 7 | **pillo.9341** | 6 | 4.3h | 936 | 0.860 | 151.9* | 34.5* | 4.7 | 5.7 | **26.2** |
| 8 | **Rojeb.4897** ⚑ | 4 | 2.1h | 629 | 0.687 | 173.4⚑ | 58.75⚑ | 3.5 | 5.5 | **22.9** |
| 9 | **Bouzouki.7598** | 12 | 9.3h | 873 | 0.545 | 100.6* | 30* | 5.3 | 7.9 | **−0.9** |

> ⚑ DH/s and CC/run imputed at pool median — player was not on Druid in any sampled section run (Jun 10, Jun 17, May 31). · \* Single Druid run sample — high per-run variance. · † Only 2 Druid runs; treat score as directional, not definitive.

DH/s and CC/run sample runs: **Jun 17** and **Jun 10 Agg** for Eliare + pillo (2-run average); **May 31 Agg** for Aeri, Astronauta, Bouzouki, Delightful Denali (single run). Conditions Out available only for Eliare (≈9.1 apps/min) and pillo (≈7.7 apps/min) — insufficient for pool scoring.

{{figure}}

---

### Key callouts

**Aeri & DraGonfly are the clear standouts on Druid.** Both exceed 2,090 HPS and 1.0 CPS — well above the next tier (Denali at 1,457 / 0.862). Both have since transitioned away from Druid (Aeri → Specter, DraGonfly → Berserker), but if either rotates back, their Druid performance benchmarks are the squad's best on record.

**Astronauta ranks 3rd but on only 2 Druid runs.** The numbers look strong (2,059 HPS, 1.142 CPS — highest cleanse rate in the group), but 6.0 deaths and 9.0 downs per run over 2 runs is too small a window to trust. Treat as promising rather than proven.

**Delightful Denali is the most reliable active Druid** in current rotation — 15 runs, solid across all dimensions, and the highest CC output (73/run) of any player with direct data. Consistent enough to trust as a primary slot.

**Eliare warrants a positioning conversation.** They lead the squad in Druid attendance (25 runs, 86.6M total healing, 47.9k cleanses) and their DH/s is strong (190.9), but 6.2 deaths and 10.0 downs per run are the worst in the pool — both more than double Aeri's numbers. Volume is there; survivability isn't.

**Bouzouki scores last.** Their 12-run Druid aggregate shows 873 HPS and 0.545 CPS — both minimums — and their May 31 downed healing (280k) was also the lowest Druid sample. Their recent switch to Specter tracks: the Druid numbers don't support keeping them in the role.
